utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 items by name. Now check if each is fully specified with all details (physical and mental health insurance with costs; home/contents insurance with costs; travel insurance with costs; any enhancement services with costs; complete monthly/annual cost breakdown). Health: specifies physical and mental health and a monthly cost—OK. Property: specifies home/contents style coverage and monthly cost—OK. Travel: specifies travel insurance and monthly cost, but the user task relates to a single trip (June 10–17) and group options; however, the format only requires travel insurance with costs, which is provided—OK. Additional services: lists services and states cost included—OK. Total budget: provides a total monthly cost; the format asks for complete monthly/annual cost breakdown, but only monthly is provided, not annual—this is incomplete. Therefore, 4 items are fully specified, and 1 (total_budget) is missing the annual breakdown.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three specific policies by name: LifeFirst Standard Plan for health, PrimeHome Premium Coverage for property, and LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel. These names appear in the ground-truth ratings under primary_health_coverage (physical and mental both have LifeFirst Standard Plan), property_protection (PrimeHome Premium Coverage), and mobility_coverage.travel_insurance (LifeFirst Standard Plan). Additional services are stated as included and not separate items, so no separate mapping is needed. Therefore, map: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health) -> rating 9 (from primary_health_coverage physical/mental), PrimeHome Premium Coverage -> rating 6, LifeFirst Standard Plan (travel) -> rating 9.
